headline: The Kiplinger Letter Says Almost None of the GDP Growth Washington Is Celebrating Actually Came From AI and for Investors the Implications Are Uncomfortable
summary: The Kiplinger Letter’s argument is uncomfortable for anyone overweight artificial intelligence: almost none of the GDP growth Washington has been celebrating actually came from AI. The mechanics are counterintuitive. When hyperscalers buy NVIDIA chips manufactured by TSMC in Taiwan and servers assembled overseas, that spending lands in the import column, which subtracts from GDP. The ...
